title: crotalaria squamigera
intro: Crotalaria squamigera, also known as the "kakadu plum" or "bush plum," is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to combat infections and promote wound healing. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. Crotalaria squamigera can be prepared as a herbal remedy through decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
